steps.title: Observe the trend and eliminate negative slopes
steps.content: Reading the scatterplot, all data points trend upward from left to right, indicating a positive linear relationship. Choices A, B, and C all have slope $-0.9$ (negative), which contradicts the data. Only choice D has positive slope.

steps.title: Calculate slope and y-intercept from sample points
steps.content:
From the scatterplot, I identify approximate points $(1, 3)$ and $(9, 10)$.
$$\text{slope} = \frac{10 - 3}{9 - 1} = \frac{7}{8} \approx 0.9$$
Using the point $(1, 3)$ with slope $0.9$:
$$3 = 0.9(1) + b$$
$$b \approx 2.1 \approx 2.2$$
The linear model is $y = 0.9x + 2.2$, which matches choice D — D is correct.
